summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a legendary 5/11/72 show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and superb 1972 jams.
review: The tape captures the band in top form – Jerry’s soaring, feedback‑laden guitar weaves with Phil’s fluid bass through what many call the longest uninterrupted Dark Star of the year. The soundboard source is crystal‑clear and full‑bodied, making the intricate interplay and the powerful second‑set jams (Sugar Magnolia, Caution, Morning Dew, Truckin') a joy to hear.

ai_rating.rationale: Over thirty five‑star reviews praise both the performance (especially Dark Star) and the pristine soundboard source; only minor audio‑quality notes appear, yielding an almost unanimous high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, full‑bodied mix with no noticeable dropouts; a few reviewers note the very first songs sound slightly off but overall the tape is crisp and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Epic length with only one brief break; the first set builds nicely and the second set erupts into a marathon Dark Star that segues into classic jams, delivering a seamless, high‑energy arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his wild, feedback‑rich tone and leading the massive Dark Star improvisation.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for subtle, intricate bass work that meshes perfectly with Jerry during the extended jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s jazzy contributions highlighted; one reviewer calls it “Keith’s fire‑breathing demon era.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (drums) cited as part of the tight, symbiotic unit driving the Dark Star’s tempo shifts.
